# Code of Conduct

## Our Commitment

We want MuseMelody to be a respectful, collaborative, and constructive space for everyone who uses, studies, contributes to, or discusses this project.

Participants in this project are expected to treat each other with respect, communicate in good faith, and focus on improving the product and codebase.

## Expected Behavior

Examples of behavior that contributes to a positive environment include:

- Using welcoming and respectful language
- Accepting constructive feedback
- Focusing on what is best for the project and its users
- Being professional in technical discussions and reviews
- Respecting different levels of experience and backgrounds

## Unacceptable Behavior

Examples of unacceptable behavior include:

- Harassment, insults, or personal attacks
- Discriminatory or exclusionary language
- Trolling, deliberate provocation, or bad-faith arguments
- Publishing private information without permission
- Any other conduct that would be considered inappropriate in a professional collaboration setting

## Enforcement

Project maintainers may remove, edit, or reject comments, commits, issues, pull requests, and other contributions that do not align with this Code of Conduct.

Repeated or severe violations may result in temporary or permanent restrictions from participating in the project.

## Reporting

If you experience or witness behavior that violates this Code of Conduct, please contact the project maintainers through the repository owner or a private communication channel when available.

## Scope

This Code of Conduct applies within the repository, issues, pull requests, discussions, and other project-related communication spaces.
